frameworks.name: Memoised Wake-Sleep (MWS)
frameworks.one_line: memoises discrete latents to avoid repeated inner-loop discrete inference

frameworks.name: wake-sleep approximate inference
frameworks.one_line: amortized inference algorithm for learning generative models with latent variables

frameworks.name: importance sampling
frameworks.one_line: learned recognition model proposes continuous variables for marginalization

frameworks.name: hybrid discrete-continuous generative models
frameworks.one_line: models combining symbolic structure with continuous parameters for perception

core_question: How can approximate inference be made effective in generative models mixing discrete and continuous latent variables?
key_assumptions: discrete and continuous latents jointly underlie observed data
memoising discrete latents reduces inner-loop inference cost
a separate recognition network can amortize continuous-variable inference
manipulations: discrete-only memoisation vs hybrid discrete+continuous handling
evaluation across GP-kernel learning and 3D scene understanding domains
key_findings: HMWS outperforms current state-of-the-art inference methods on tested tasks
learned recognition model enables importance-sampled marginalization over continuous variables
memoisation of discrete variables alleviates the repeated inner-loop inference burden
interpretations: a principled hybrid scheme handles discrete-continuous interfaces more effectively than prior approaches
combining memoisation with learned continuous proposals is a viable inference strategy
